verdict.answer_first: A: sentence 1 gives the default plan: "recurring subscriptions + Stripe-hosted Checkout" and "recommend by default"; B starts with assumptions and stack.
verdict.decisiveness: A commits cleaner to one path: "what I'd recommend by default"; B dilutes with "Node/TypeScript-ish backend" and more caveats.
verdict.fabrication: B invents an unstated stack assumption: "Node/TypeScript-ish backend"; A also assumes subscriptions/Checkout but frames it as "most common SaaS case."
verdict.actionability: A is easier to execute: concrete fields like "stripe_subscription_id" and endpoint names "POST /api/checkout" / "POST /api/stripe/webhook" plus failure-path tests.
